title: turnera annectens
intro: Turnera annectens, also known as West Indian gooseberry or moonshine, is a plant species within the Turneraceae family, native to the tropical regions of Central and South America. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its potential medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Turnera annectens include relieving symptoms of anxiety and depression, reducing inflammation, and improving sleep quality. Turnera annectens can be prepared as a herbal tea or infused oil, and its leaves and stems are often used in traditional remedies.
